content.text: ## Where shebang comments are handled ### Lexer: recognizes `#!...` as a dedicated token File: **`/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Lexer.java`** In `nextDefault()` the lexer treats `#` specially: ```java case '#' -> { if (lookahead == '!') { yield lexShebang(); } else { yield lexStringStartPounds(); } } ``` And `lexShebang()` consumes characters until newline/CR/EOF and returns `Token.SHEBANG`: ```java private Token lexShebang() { do { nextChar(); } while (lookahead != '\n' && lookahead != '\r' && lookahead != EOF); return Token.SHEBANG; } ``` Token definition: File: **`/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Token.java`** ```java SHEBANG, ``` ### Parser: shebang is skipped like a comment/semicolon File: **`/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Parser.java`** The parser’s token pump `forceNext()` ignores SHEBANG (as well as comments + semicolons): ```java private FullToken forceNext() { var tk = lexer.next(); precededBySemicolon = false; while (tk == Token.LINE_COMMENT || tk == Token.BLOCK_COMMENT || tk == Token.SEMICOLON || tk == Token.SHEBANG) { precededBySemicolon = precededBySemicolon || tk == Token.SEMICOLON; tk = lexer.next(); } return new FullToken(tk, lexer.span(), lexer.newLinesBetween); } ``` So today, **shebang is effectively treated as whitespace/comment anywhere**, not specifically “only at file start”. There’s also a core test asserting this behavior: File: **`/testbed/pkl-core/src/test/kotlin/org/pkl/core/parser/ShebangTest.kt`** ```kotlin #!/usr/local/bin/pkl x = 1 ``` ## How “file-start” is detected (currently) There is **no explicit “start-of-file” / BOL marker token** in the lexer or parser. The only implicit indicator is: - Lexer fields: `cursor`, `sCursor`, and `newLinesBetween` (counted while skipping whitespace in `nextDefault()`). - Parser initialization: `init(source)` immediately calls `forceNext()` once; `parseModule()` uses the first returned token’s `spanLookahead` as the module’s `start`. File-start behavior is therefore inferred by **position (`lexer.cursor` / spans)** rather than a formal “START_OF_FILE” token. ## Files + functions you’d modify (depending on desired behavior) ### If you want shebang to be valid **only at the very beginning of the file** Primary place to enforce: **Parser** (because lexer currently can return SHEBANG anywhere) - **`/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Parser.java`** - `forceNext()` — currently skips `Token.SHEBANG` unconditionally; you’d change logic to only skip it when it’s the first meaningful thing in the file (typically: first token emitted at/near charIndex 0, possibly allowing BOM/whitespace before it if desired). - Potentially `init(String source)` — if you want to track a “haven’t consumed any non-trivia yet” boolean. Alternative enforcement: **Lexer** - **`/testbed/pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Lexer.java`** - `next()` / `nextDefault()` / `lexShebang()` — could be changed to only lex shebang when `sCursor==0` (or when all prior chars are whitespace/BOM), otherwise treat `#` as string-pound-start. ### If you want more explicit file-start handling for multiple features - Add an explicit “atStartOfFile” concept in the parser (likely in `Parser.init` + `forceNext`) or in `Lexer` (tracking whether any token has been produced yet). Today, nothing like that exists. These are the key modification points because **shebang is produced in `Lexer.nextDefault()` and discarded in `Parser.forceNext()`**, and “file start” is currently only represented implicitly by spans/cursors.

package org.pkl.core.parser

import org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
import org.pkl.core.Evaluator
import org.pkl.core.ModuleSource

class ShebangTest {
  private val evaluator = Evaluator.preconfigured()

  @Test
  fun `shebang is ignored`() {
    val module =
      evaluator.evaluate(
        ModuleSource.text(
          """
          #!/usr/local/bin/pkl
          x = 1
        """
            .trimIndent()
        )
      )

    assertThat(module.properties["x"]).isEqualTo(1L)
  }
}

content.text: Fixed shebang parsing so `#!...` is only treated as a shebang when it is the very first token in the file. Changes: - `pkl-parser/src/main/java/org/pkl/parser/Parser.java`: only skips `Token.SHEBANG` when `lexer.span().charIndex() == 0`; otherwise it is left to be parsed (and will error in non-initial positions). - Left lexer behavior unchanged (still lexes `#!` as `SHEBANG`), but parser now enforces the “start-of-file only” rule. Testing: - Added a failing regression test in `pkl-core/src/test/kotlin/org/pkl/core/parser/ShebangTest.kt`, confirmed it reproduced the bug, then removed it as requested. - Re-ran `./gradlew :pkl-core:test --tests org.pkl.core.parser.ShebangTest` successfully after the fix and cleanup.
